nodes.label: AI as collaborative solution refiner
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to use it collaboratively, for content tasks such as refining blog posts

nodes.label: AI as rubber-ducky troubleshooting plus
nodes.grounding_span: rubber duck debugging, when I'm stuck on something complicated and need to find a way forward

nodes.label: AI as quick guidance and short coding assistant
nodes.grounding_span: short time saving things rather than big refactors or features

nodes.label: AI code quality and reliability
nodes.label_negative: AI unreliability for large code tasks
nodes.grounding_span: the results of asking AI to do large feature work autonomously aren't good enough

nodes.label: AI as skill development enabler
nodes.grounding_span: developers that use AI tools will replace developers who don't, but I don't think that AI will replace developers

nodes.label: Quality of output
nodes.grounding_span: a collaborative approach just results in the best quality of output

nodes.label: Continuous learning and relevance
nodes.grounding_span: standing still in the technology sector is going backwards so you have to continuously update your skill sets

nodes.label: Frustration with AI for coding
nodes.grounding_span: it was just making dumb mistakes and fixing one led to more problems, very frustrating

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI as a tool
nodes.grounding_span: I don't know if it ever felt satisfying

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: developers that use AI tools will replace developers who don't

nodes.label: Collaborative iterative refinement — works back and forth with AI
nodes.grounding_span: we went back and forth a few times and started turning out something good

nodes.label: Pragmatic cost-benefit evaluation
nodes.grounding_span: often it's quicker just to write the thing myself that it is to debug the AI generator code
